title: [ipv6_addr.c:239-249] snprintf format uses lowercase hex but LICHEN may expect uppercase
description:
Round 2 gap analysis: Missed because formatting consistency wasn't checked.

In lichen_ipv6_addr_to_str at lines 239-249:
```c
int ret = snprintf(buf, buflen,
         "%02x%02x:%02x%02x:%02x%02x:%02x%02x:"
         "%02x%02x:%02x%02x:%02x%02x:%02x%02x",
         addr->s6_addr[0], addr->s6_addr[1], ...
```

The format uses lowercase hex (%02x) which produces 'fe80:0000:...' format. The header comment in ipv6_addr.h line 97-99 says 'uncompressed lowercase hex format' so this is intentional and documented.

However, RFC 5952 section 4.3 recommends lowercase, so this is actually correct. Not a bug, just confirming the code matches the documentation.

No issue - code is correct per RFC 5952.
close_reason: False positive - code is correct per RFC 5952 which recommends lowercase. This was filed mistakenly.
